info.name: Thyrocare B2C APIs
info.description:
The primary goal of the API documentation is to provide comprehensive guidance to clients, enabling them to comprehend the process from order booking to reporting, and to facilitate the consumption of services through the available methods.
This document encompasses all authentication-protected APIs, accompanied by sample requests and responses, CURL commands, and error codes.

Base URLs:

  • Production Environment : https://api.thyrocare.com

  • Staging Environment : https://api-sandbox.thyrocare.com

To start using the B2C APIs, you need to -

  • You must use a valid token to send requests to the API endpoints. You can get your token from Login API.

  • The API has rate limits.

  • Our APIs operate on TLS v1.2 or above.

  • The API returns request responses in JSON format.

  • All the timestamps in the responses are in UTC (TZ format). Please convert them as per you preferred time zone.

Common Headers:

Header Name Required Description
Partner-Id Yes Unique partner identifier
Request-Id Yes Unique identifier for the request
API-Version Yes API version information
Client-Type Yes Specifies the type of client (e.g., web, mobile)
Content-Type Yes Must be application/json

HTTP Status Codes

Status Code Status Name Description
200 OK The request succeeded.
201 CREATED The resource was successfully created.
400 BAD_REQUEST Invalid request (e.g., malformed syntax, missing parameters).
401 UNAUTHORIZED Authentication failed or not provided.
403 FORBIDDEN Insufficient permissions to access the resource.
404 RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND The requested resource does not exist.
405 METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED The HTTP method is not supported for this endpoint.
409 CONFLICT Request conflicts with existing data (e.g., duplicate entry).
415 UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE The request media type (e.g., Content-Type) is not supported.
422 UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY Request validation failed (e.g., semantic errors in payload).
429 TOO_MANY_REQUESTS Rate limit exceeded.
500 INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR Unexpected server-side error.
503 SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE The server is temporarily unavailable (e.g., maintenance).
4xx CLIENT_ERROR Generic client-side error (if status code doesn’t match known ones).
5xx SERVER_ERROR Generic server-side error (if status code doesn’t match known ones).

Error

Thyrocare uses conventional HTTP response codes to indicate the success or failure of an API request as mentioned above. All error responses follow this format:

{
    "errors": [
        {
            "code": "BAD_REQUEST",
            "message": "user with given username and entity type not found"
        }
    ]
}

Common Error Codes

These error codes may appear in the errorCode field of error responses:

Error Code Description
INVALID_REQUEST The request is invalid (generic fallback).
PARAMETER_MISSING A required parameter is missing or empty.
VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE A parameter value violates length/numeric constraints.
UNEXPECTED_VALUE A parameter contains disallowed characters/values.
INVALID_DATE_FORMAT A date/time field has an incorrect format.
INVALID_VALUE A parameter value is invalid (generic).
DUPLICATE_ENTRY The request attempts to create a duplicate resource.
BAD_REQUEST Invalid request structure (HTTP 400).
UNAUTHORIZED Missing or invalid authentication (HTTP 401).
FORBIDDEN Insufficient permissions (HTTP 403).
RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND The requested resource does not exist (HTTP 404).
METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED Unsupported HTTP method (HTTP 405).
CONFLICT Data conflict (e.g., duplicate entry) (HTTP 409).
UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE Invalid Content-Type header (HTTP 415).
UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY Payload validation failed (HTTP 422).
TOO_MANY_REQUESTS Rate limit exceeded (HTTP 429).
INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR Server-side error (HTTP 500).
SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE Server temporarily unavailable (HTTP 503).
ATTEMPT_LIMIT_EXCEED

Rate and usage limits

API access rate limits apply at API key basis. The limit is different for different APIs. If you exceed this limit, your request will return an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests status code.

Rate Limit Exceeded response

{
    "errors": [
        {
            "code": "ATTEMPT_LIMIT_EXCEED",
            "message": "TOO MANY ATTEMPTS. PLEASE TRY AFTER 5 MINUTES."
        }
    ]
}

Security

  • Always transmit credentials over HTTPS

  • Store the received token securely

  • Include token in Authorization: Bearer header for subsequent requests

item.name: Authentication

request.description:
Description:

This API authenticates users and generates a JWT token for accessing protected endpoints. It validates credentials against the system and returns a time-bound access token. Used by partners to establish authenticated sessions. This API validates credentials against strict formatting rules and implements security measures like rate limiting.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Name Required Description
Entity-Type Yes Specifies the entity type (Must be DSA)

Request Payload

Field Validations

Parameter Type Required Validation Rules
username String Yes - 10 digits exactly
- Numeric only
- Cannot start with 0
- Valid mobile number format
password String Yes - Minimum 6 characters
- Cannot be blank

Responses

Fields:

Field Type Description
token String JWT token for authenticated requests. Valid until exp claim in token.

response.name: RateLimitExceeded

request.description:
Description:

Invalidates the current authentication token, terminating the user session. Must be called when users log out of the application.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer

Responses

Fields:

Field Type Description
message String Confirmation of logout action

Notes

  1. Requires valid JWT from Login API

  2. Token becomes invalid immediately after success

  3. Client should clear local token storage after calling

  4. No request body required

Description:

Generates and sends a One-Time Password (OTP) to the registered mobile number and email for authentication. Used during user registration, password reset, or sensitive operations.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Entity-Type Yes Must be DSA

Request Payload

Field Validations

Parameter Type Required
mobile String No
countryCode String No
emailId String No
username String Yes

Responses

Fields:

Field Type Description
message String Confimation of OTP generation action

Notes

  1. username will be received from Thyrocare team during registration

  2. OTP expiry will be expired in 5 minutes

  3. OTP will be sent on registered mobile number against username

  4. OTP can be generated five times in 5 minutes.

response.name: RateLimitExceeded

request.description:
Description:

Verifies the OTP sent to the user's registered mobile/email and returns an authentication token upon successful validation. This is the second step in the two-factor authentication process.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Entity-Type Yes Must be DSA

Request Payload

Field Validations

Parameter Type Required Validation Rules
username String Yes - 10 digits
- Numeric only
- No leading zero
otp String Yes - Minimum 4 characters

Responses

Fields:

Field Type Description
token String JWT for authenticated requests. Valid until expiration.

Notes

  1. OTP is typically valid for 5 minutes

  2. The username must match the one used in OTP generation

  3. Store the JWT token securely after successful verification

  4. There is a limit for OTP generation- 5 attempts before temporary lock

item.description:
The DSA APIs uses token for authentication. You must include a token in each request to the Postman API with the Authorization request header.

Your API keys carry many privileges, so be sure to keep them secure! Do not share your secret token in publicly accessible areas such as GitHub, client-side code, and so forth.

All API requests must be made over HTTPS. Calls made over plain HTTP will fail. API requests without authentication will also fail.

If Authorization header is missing, malformed, or invalid, you will receive an HTTP 403 Forbidden response code.

request.description:
Description:
Calculates the detailed pricing breakdown for diagnostic tests and offers in the cart, including MRP, discounts, incentives, and additional charges. Used to display final pricing before order confirmation.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Request Body

Root Object

Field Type Required Description
patients Array Yes Min 1 patient
isReportHardCopyRequired Boolean Yes Whether hard copy is needed

Patient Object

Field Type Required Validation Values
name String Yes Non-blank -
gender Enum Yes - MALE, FEMALE
age Integer Yes Min 1 -
ageType Enum Yes - YEAR, MONTH, DAY
items Array of object Yes Min 1 item -

Item Object

Field Type Required Validation
id String Yes Non-blank
type Enum Yes Non-null. Valid values are: [SSKU, OFFER, PSKU, OSKU]
name String Yes Non-blank
rate Object Yes -

Rate Object

Field Type Required Validation
currency String Yes Non-blank
mrp String Yes Non-blank, numeric

Response Fields

Root Object

Field Type Description
patients Array[Patient] List of patients
rates CartRate Pricing details

Patient Object

Field Type Description
id String Patient identifier
name String Patient name
age Integer Patient age
ageType Enum Age unit (YEAR,MONTH,DAY)
gender Enum MALE, FEMALE
address String Patient address
contactNumber String Mobile number
email String Email address
items Array[Item] Test/offer items
samples Array[Sample] Sample details

Item Object

Field Type Description
id String Item ID
type String Item type
name String Display name
origin OriginInfo Source details
tests Array[Test] Test details
price OrderDetailsPrice Item pricing

CartRate Object

Field Type Description
currency String Always "INR"
netPayableAmount String Final payable amount
totalMrp String Sum of all MRPs
totalSellingPrice String After discounts
totalCashback String Total cashback
totalDiscount String Total discounts

This field is only applicable if you send something in the "discounts" array in downstream.
totalCharges String Sum of all charges
cashbacks Array Cashback details.

Only Cashbacks Coupons authorized and shared by the Thyrocare team can be used here.
incentives Incentives Incentive details
discounts Array[Discount] Applied discounts.

Only Discount Coupons authorized and shared by the Thyrocare team can be used here.
charges Array[Charge] Breakdown of charges

Charge Object

Field Type Description Values
type String Charge type TESTING_CHARGES, HARD_COPY_REPORT_CHARGES
amount String Charge amount -
label String Display label -

Incentives Object

Field Type Description
total String Gross incentive value
passon String Passed to customer

This indicates the total amount in rupees which will be passed on to the end users, at the complete order level, not at the individual patient level.
net String Net incentive

Discount Object

Field Type Description Values
type Enum Discount type COUPON, LOYALTY_DISCOUNT, etc.

Only Discount Coupons authorized and shared by the Thyrocare team can be used here.
amount String Discount value -

Sample Object

Field Type Description
sampleType String Sample type
barcode String Sample barcode
status String Collection status

Notes

  1. Always check netPayableAmount for final amount

  2. charges array will always contain:

    • At least one TESTING_CHARGES entry

    • Optional HARD_COPY_REPORT_CHARGES (₹75) when applicable

request.description:
Description

Retrieves the complete catalog of available tests, profiles, and offers with their details including pricing, sample requirements, and technical specifications.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Query Parameters

Query Parameter Required Type Description
minPrice Optional Number Minimum price filter for test packages/products. Use to filter results starting from this price. Default value is 0.
maxPrice Optional Number Maximum price filter for test packages/products. Use to filter results up to this price.
gender Optional String Gender of the patient. Common values: MALE, FEMALE. Some tests might be gender-specific.
page Optional Number Page number for paginated results. Starts typically from 1.
pageSize Optional Number Number of items per page. Helps in controlling result size for pagination. Default value is 10.

Response Fields

Field Type Description
isLastPage Boolean Indicates whether the current page is the last one.
nextPage Integer Page number for the next page of results. Null if no further pages.
skuList Array of Object List of catalog items (PSKU/SSKU/OFFER).

SkuList Object

Field Type Description
id String Unique identifier of the SKU item which need to passed carefully while booking the orders.

Please use this in the create order API.
name String Display name of the SKU
aliases Array of string List of alternative names for the SKU. Can be empty.
type String SKU type:
• PSKU – Primary SKU (an individual test)
• SSKU – Secondary SKU (a package with grouped tests)
• OFFER – Promotional or client-specific bundled SKU.
noOfTestsIncluded Integer Number of individual diagnostic tests included.
testsIncluded Array of object List of objects containing test names included in this SKU.
rate Object Pricing and incentive information (see below).
beneficiaries Object Rules for number of people the SKU can be ordered for.
flags Object Metadata about fasting, collection type, and payment.
categories Array of string Reserved for future use – diagnostic categories or tags. Empty array in this payload.

TestsIncluded Object

Field Type Description
name String Name of the diagnostic test.
groupName String Name of the group of this Diagnostic Paramter
Tip: This can be used to show the group header under which this test parameter lie.

Rate Object

Field Type Description
currency String Currency code (e.g., INR).
listingPrice String Maximum retail price of the SKU.
sellingPrice String Final price after discount.
discountPercentage String Percentage of discount applied.
notationalIncentive String Theoretical value of Incentive amount.

Beneficiaries Object

Field Type Description
min String Minimum number of beneficiaries allowed per order.
multiple String Multiples in which SKU can be ordered.
max String Maximum number of beneficiaries allowed per order.
canAddPostOrder Boolean Indicates if beneficiary can be added after placing order. May be null.

Flags Object

Field Type Description
isFastingRequired Boolean Indicates if fasting is required before test collection.
isHomeCollectible Boolean Indicates if the sample can be collected from home.
isPostpaid Boolean Indicates if payment can be made post service. (Optional)

Categories (Array)

Field Type Description
— Object Reserved for category metadata (empty in this response).

Notes

  1. Data Freshness:

    • Clients should cache responses for better performance

  2. Pricing:

    • MRPs are fixed but incentives may vary by partner

    • Final payable amount is sellingPrice

  3. Response Size:

    • Complete catalog may contain 100+ items

    • Response compression is recommended

request.description:
Description:

Checks service availability for different tests in specified pincodes. Used to validate whether diagnostic services can be offered in a particular area.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Responses

Fields:

Field Type Description Possible Values
serviceTypes Array Service availability groups -
type String Service category PATHOLOGY, ECG, MER, ALL
pincodes Integer Servicable pincodes 6-digit pincodes

Service Types

Value Description
PATHOLOGY Regular blood/urine tests
ECG Electrocardiogram services
MER Medical equipment rental
ALL All available services

Notes

  1. Pincode validation:

    • Must be 6-digit Indian pincodes

    • No alphabets or special characters

  2. Cache recommendations:

    • Cache responses for 24 hours

    • Refresh on user location change

request.description:
Description:

Retrieves available time slots for home sample collection based on location (pincode), appointment date, and test requirements. Used to schedule diagnostic tests.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Request Payload

Field Validations

Parameter Type Required Validation Rules
appointmentDate String Yes yyyy-MM-dd format
pincode String Yes 6-digit numeric
patients Array Yes Min 1 patient

Patient Object:

Field Type Required Values
name String Yes -
gender Enum Yes MALE, FEMALE, OTHER
age Integer Yes Min 1
ageType Enum Yes YEAR, MONTH, DAY
items Array Yes Min 1 test

Test Item:

Field Type Required Description
id String Yes Test code.
Please pick the "id" from the response of the /catalog API and pass here.
type Enum Yes Please pick the "type" from the response of the /catalog API and pass here.
Valid values are: SSKU, OFFER, PSKU, and OSKU
name String Yes Please pick the "name" from the response of the /catalog API and pass here.
Test name

Responses

Fields:

Field Type Description
timeZone String Always IST (+5:30 Asia/Kolkata)
appointmentDate String Echoes requested date
slots Array Available time windows
id String Slot identifier for booking
startTime/endTime String 24-hour format (HH:mm) and are based on the timeZone specified in the response.

Notes

  1. Date Rules:

    • Minimum: Current date

    • Maximum: Usually 7 days in future

  2. Multi-Patient:

    • All tests must be serviceable in same slot

    • Returns only common available slots

  3. Time Format:

    • All times as per mentioned format

request.description:
Description

Creates a new diagnostic test order with patient details, test items, collection address, and appointment information.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Request Body

Field Type Required Validation Description
address Address Yes - Collection address details
email String Yes Valid email format Contact email
contactNumber String Yes +[country code]-[10 digit number] Primary contact number
appointment Object Yes - Collection schedule
origin Object Yes - Source/origin details
referredBy Object Yes - Referring doctor info
paymentDetails Object Yes - Payment configuration
attributes Object Yes - Order metadata
config Object No - Communication preferences
patients Array of object Yes Min 1 patient Patient details
price Object Yes - Pricing information
orderOptions Object No - Special order flags

Address Object

Field Type Required Description
houseNo String No House/building number
street String Yes Street name
addressLine1 String Yes Primary address line
addressLine2 String Yes Secondary address line
landmark String No Nearby landmark
city String Yes City name
state String Yes State name
country String Yes Country name
pincode Integer Yes Postal code

⚠️ Note:
1. The maximum allowed address length is 175 characters.
2. The total address length is calculated as: length of houseNo + street + addressLine1 + addressLine2 + landmark + city + state + country + pincode

Appointment Object

Field Type Required Validation Description
date String Yes yyyy-MM-dd format Collection date
startTime String Yes HH:mm 24-hour format Collection time
timeZone Enum Yes Valid timezone (see below) Timezone for appointment

TimeZone Possible Values

Value Description Region/City
IST Asia/Kolkata Indian Standard Time
GMT GMT Greenwich Mean Time
UTC UTC Coordinated Universal Time

Patient Object

Field Type Required Description
name String Yes Patient full name
gender Enum Yes MALE or FEMALE
age Integer Yes Minimum value: 1
ageType Enum Yes YEAR, MONTH, or DAY
contactNumber String Yes Patient contact
email String Yes Valid email format
items Array of object Yes Tests/profiles/offers ordered for this patient
Currently we do not support booking different SKUs for different patients, inside a multipatient order. Hence all patient objects should have same SKU lists.
attributes Map No Additional metadata
documents Array of object No Supporting documents

Patients.attributes Object

Field Type Description
ulcUniqueCode String Unique lab code (if any).
patientAddress String Collection address or facility for patient.
externalPatientId String External patient ID reference.

Item Object

Field Type Required Description
id String Yes Test/profile identifier
This should be the "id" field from SKU Object from the Catalog API.
type Enum Yes Valid values are: SSKU, OFFER, PSKU, and OSKU
name String Yes Display name
origin object Yes Source information

Origin Object

Field Type Description
platform String Must be DSA-PARTNER
appId String App identifier.
portalType String Portal type: e.g., B2C
enteredBy String User ID or name who initiated the booking.
source String Origin source/description: Use B2C MIDDLEWARE API

Patients.documents Object

Field Type Description
attributes String / Object Optional metadata.
documentPath String Path or reference to document file.
documentType String Type/category of document.

ReferredBy Object

Field Type Description
doctorId String Unique ID of referring doctor (optional).
doctorName String Name of referring doctor.

PaymentDetails Object

Field Type Description
payType String Valid values arePOSTPAID, PREPAID

Attributes Object

Field Type Description
remarks String Free text remarks (if any).
phleboNotes String A note for phlebo/technician
campId String / null ID of health camp (optional).
isReportHardCopyRequired Boolean Whether physical report copy is required.
refOrderNo String Reference/order number from external system. Maximum 25 alphanumeric characters are allowed.
collectionType String Sample collection type: HOME_COLLECTION.
alertMessage Array List of alerts (can be empty or strings).

Config.communication Object

Field Type Description
shareReport Boolean Whether to share diagnostic reports.
shareReceipt Boolean Whether to share billing receipt.
shareModes Object Preferred channels for communication (see below).

ShareModes Object

Field Type Description
whatsapp Boolean Share via WhatsApp if true.
email Boolean Share via email if true.

Price Object

Field Type Description
discounts Array Applied discount info (code and type).
Only Discount Coupons authorized and shared by the Thyrocare team can be used here.
incentivePasson Object Incentive details passed to end-user (see below).

This indicates the total amount in rupees which will be passed on to the end users, at the complete order level, not at the individual patient level.

IncentivePasson Object

Field Type Description
type String Incentive types: FLAT and PERCENT.
Currently, we support only FLAT, where the amount should be passed in the value parameter.
value String Value of incentive.
This is the incentive passon value of the complete order (not for individual patients).

orderOptions Object

Field Type Description
isPdpcOrder Boolean Indicates if this is a PDPC (Pre-Defined Patient Configuration) order.

Response

Success Response (201 Created)

Field Type Description
orderNo String Unique order identifier (starts with "VL")

Notes

  1. Order Number:

    • Always starts with "VL" followed by alphanumeric characters

  2. Validation Rules:

    • Date formats strictly enforced (yyyy-MM-dd for dates, HH:mm for times)

    • Contact numbers require country code prefix (+XX-XXXXXXXXXX)

    • Aarogyam profile is only available with offer booking

    • The maximum allowed address length is 175 characters.*_The total address length is calculated as: *_\length of houseNo + street + addressLine1 + addressLine2 + landmark + city + state + country + pincode

  3. Time Handling:

    • All times interpreted according to specified timezone

    • Appointment slots subject to availability

  4. Idempotency:

    • Identical refOrderNo return same order number

  5. Multipatient Orders:

    1. Currently we do not support booking different SKUs for different patients, inside a multipatient order. Hence the "items" list inside every "patient" object should be same.

    2. If you send different SKUs for different patients in the same order then you will receive an error with message: "Order creation failed - Booking different items for different patients is not allowed. Please book the same items for all patients."

  6. Incentive Passon:

    1. Currently, we support only FLAT, where the amount should be passed in the value parameter.

    2. Support for PERCENT will be released soon.

request.description:
Description

Retrieves comprehensive details for a specific order including tracking information, patient details, and pricing. Supports optional field inclusion via query parameters.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Path Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
order_id String Yes Unique identifier of the order

Query Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
include String No Comma-separated fields to include (tracking,items,price)

Response

Response Fields

Field Type Description
orderId String Unique order identifier
orderType String Type of order (ILS/etc.)
refOrderNo String Reference/order number
status String Current order status
clientCode String Partner/client reference code
appointmentDate String Timestamp of sample collection or appointment Date and time
email String Patient email (nullable)
contactNumber String Patient contact number
address Address Collection address details
orderTracking Array[OrderTracking] Status history timeline
referredBy ReferredBy Doctor referral information
paymentDetails PaymentDetails Payment information
price OrderDetailsPrice Complete pricing breakdown
patients Array[Patient] Patient details and tests
alertMessage String System alerts/warnings
parentOrderid String For CTL orders, this field contains the orderNo of the original order
that was cancelled and replaced by this CTL order. For other (normal) orders, parentOrderNo
will be the same as orderNo.

Address Object

Field Type Description
houseNo String House/building number
street String Street name
addressLine1 String Primary address line
addressLine2 String Secondary address line
landmark String Nearby landmark
city String City name
state String State name
country String Country name
pincode Integer Postal code

OrderTracking Object

Field Type Description
status String Status event (ORDER_PLACED, etc.)
timestamp String ISO-8601 timestamp of event
attributes Map Additional event metadata

ReferredBy Object

Field Type Description
doctorId String Doctor identifier
doctorName String Doctor full name

PaymentDetails Object

Field Type Description
unpaidAmount String Outstanding balance
paidAmount String Amount already paid
payType Enum POSTPAID or PREPAID

OrderDetailsPrice Object

Field Type Description
totalMrp String Maximum retail price
totalDiscount String Total discounts applied
totalSellingPrice String Price after discounts
netPayableAmount String Final payable amount
totalCharges String Additional charges
totalCashback String Cashback earned
incentives Incentives Partner incentives
cashbacks Array Cashback details
discounts Array[Discount] Applied discounts
charges Array[Charge] Additional charges

Patient Object

Field Type Description
id String Patient identifier
name String Patient full name
age Integer Patient age
ageType String Age unit (YEAR/MONTH/DAY)
gender String Patient gender
address Address Patient address
contactNumber String Patient contact
email String Patient email
items Array[Item] Tests/profiles ordered
samples Array Sample details
ctlOrderIds Array[String] If an order is cancelled and CTL replacement orders are created, this
field contains a list of the orderNo(s) of those CTL orders. For example, when you fetch the
order summary of a cancelled order, this field will list all CTL orders generated from it.

Order Processing Stages by Status

Status Description
YET TO ASSIGN / Y Order placed but not yet assigned to any technician (Phlebo).
ASSIGNED Assigned to a technician (Phlebo).
ACCEPTED Technician has accepted the order.
STARTED Technician has started the order (after acceptance).
ARRIVED Technician has arrived at the customer's location.
CONFIRMED Sample collected, payment received, details confirmed, and barcode scanned.
SERVICED Sample collection is completed and marked serviced by technician.
PARTIAL SERVICED Sample collected for some patients (in multi-patient order), rest are pending.
RESCHEDULED Appointment has been rescheduled to a new date/time.
CANCELLED The order has been cancelled.
FIX APPOINTMENT Appointment fixed as per customer's request (for a lead order).
DONE Reports have been generated for the order.
REQUEST TO RELEASE Technician has requested to release the order from their task list.
RELEASED Order released from technician’s task list (bucket).
PERSUASION OMS Ops team is following up with the customer to fix an appointment.
CALLBACK Customer has requested a callback.
CANCELLATIONREQUEST Technician or customer has requested to cancel the order.
CANCELTEST Status for CTL orders specifically marked for cancellation.
CHARBI PUSHED No technician available, so order is handed off to a franchise technician.
LEAD An order created without any test/product attached.
HCL ORDER Order booked under the HCL flow/category.
LAB Sample has been received/imported into the lab system.
REPORTED Final report(s) have been released for the order.

Notes

  1. Field Inclusion:

    • Use include parameter to optimize payload size

    • Multiple fields: include=tracking,price,items

  2. Time Zones:

    • All timestamps are in UTC

    • Convert to local timezone for display

  3. If the order is rescheduled, please refer to the appointmentDate parameter in the response for the latest/rescheduled date and time.

request.description:
Description

Retrieves a paginated list of orders with filtering and sorting capabilities. Allows partners to view their order history within a specified date range.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Query Parameters

Parameter Type Required Default Description
startDate Date Yes - Start date for filtering (yyyy-MM-dd)
endDate Date Yes - End date for filtering (yyyy-MM-dd)
sortBy Enum No ORDER_ID Field to sort by (see SortBy value)
sortOrder Enum No DESC Sort direction (ASC/DESC)
pageSize Integer No 10 Number of orders per page

** Pagination Support is being built inside this API, currently we do not have this support.

SortBy Possible Values

Value Description
ORDER_ID Order reference number (This is not live until May 2026, this support will be made after that).
BOOKING_DATE Date when order was created
APPOINTMENT_DATE Scheduled collection date
CONTACT_NUMBER Patient contact number

SortType Possible Values

Value Description
ASC Ascending order
DESC Descending order

Response Details

Field Type Description
isLastPage Boolean Indicates whether the current page is the last page of results.
orders Array of Object List of order objects.

Orders Object

Field Type Description
orderId String Unique order identifier
bookingDate String ISO-8601 timestamp of order creation
clientCode String Partner/client reference code
status String Current order status
orderType String Type of order
appointmentDate String Scheduled collection timestamp
email String Patient email (nullable)
contactNumber String Patient contact number
referredBy Object Doctor referral info
vialNumber String Sample vial identifier (nullable)
alertMessage Array of String System alerts/warnings
address Object Collection address details (nullable)
patients Array of Object Patient details
price Object Pricing information

ReferredBy Object

Field Type Description
doctorId String Doctor identifier (nullable)
doctorName String Doctor name

Address Object

Field Type Description
houseNo String House/building number
street String Street name
addressLine1 String Primary address line
addressLine2 String Secondary address line
landmark String Nearby landmark
city String City name
state String State name
country String Country name
pincode Integer Postal code

Patient Object

Field Type Description
name String Patient full name
gender String Patient gender
contactNumber String Patient contact
email String Patient email
age Integer Patient age
ageType String Age unit (YEAR/MONTH/DAY)
attributes Map Additional patient metadata
items Array of Object Tests/profiles ordered

Item Object

Field Type Description
id String Test identifier
type String Item type (TEST/PROFILE)
name String Display name
origin OriginInfo Source information
tests Array of Object Component tests
price Object Item pricing

Price Object

Field Type Description
totalMrp String Maximum retail price
totalDiscount String Total discounts applied
totalSellingPrice String Final price after discounts
netPayableAmount String Amount actually paid
totalCharges String Additional charges
totalCashback String Cashback earned
incentives Object Partner incentives
orderOptions Object Available actions

Incentives Object

Field Type Description
total String Total incentive value
passon String Customer discount portion
net String Partner earnings

OrderOptions Object

Field Type Description
isOrderDeletable Boolean If order can be cancelled
isPatientInfoEditable Boolean If patient details can be updated
isReportReady Boolean If reports are available

Notes

  1. Pagination:

    • Use isLastPage to determine if more requests are needed.

    • Pagination support is being built into the API, but we will have cursor based or ID psaed pagination, more details to be added.

request.description:
Description

Allows partners to cancel an existing order by providing a cancellation reason. This operation is irreversible and will trigger notifications to relevant parties.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Request Body

Field Type Required Validation Description
reasonKey Enum Yes Valid enum value Categorized reason for cancellation
reasonText String Yes Min 1 character Detailed explanation for cancellation

ReasonKey Possible Values

Value Description
CUSTOMER_REQUEST Customer initiated reschedule request
SERVICE_PROVIDER_REQUEST Service provider requested reschedule
SERVICE_PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE Service provider unavailable
SERVICE_PROVIDER_NOT_AVAILABLE Alternative phrasing for provider unavailability
SERVICE_PROVIDER_CANCELLED Provider cancelled original appointment
SERVICE_PROVIDER_RESCHEDULED Provider initiated reschedule
CUSTOMER_UNAVAILABLE Customer unavailable for original slot
CUSTOMER_CANCELLED Customer cancelled original appointment
CUSTOMER_RESCHEDULED Customer initiated reschedule
OTHER Reason not covered by other categories

Notes

  1. Reason Requirements:

    • reasonText is included in customer notifications

  2. Audit Trail:

    • All cancellations are logged with timestamps and reasons

    • Frequent cancellations may trigger partner reviews

request.description:
Description

Allows partners to reschedule an existing order's sample collection appointment by specifying a new date/time and providing a reason for rescheduling.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Path Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
order_id String Yes The unique identifier of the order to reschedule

Request Body

Field Type Required Validation Description
appointmentDate String Yes Format: yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm New appointment date and time in 24-hour format
reasonKey Enum Yes Valid enum value Categorized reason for rescheduling
reasonText String Yes Min 1 character Detailed explanation for rescheduling

ReasonKey Possible Values

Value Description
CUSTOMER_REQUEST Customer initiated reschedule request
SERVICE_PROVIDER_REQUEST Service provider requested reschedule
SERVICE_PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE Service provider unavailable
SERVICE_PROVIDER_NOT_AVAILABLE Alternative phrasing for provider unavailability
SERVICE_PROVIDER_CANCELLED Provider cancelled original appointment
SERVICE_PROVIDER_RESCHEDULED Provider initiated reschedule
CUSTOMER_UNAVAILABLE Customer unavailable for original slot
CUSTOMER_CANCELLED Customer cancelled original appointment
CUSTOMER_RESCHEDULED Customer initiated reschedule
OTHER Reason not covered by other categories

Notes

  1. Rescheduling Rules:

    • New slot must be within next 7 days from current date.

  2. Time Handling:

    • All times are interpreted as IST and should be in mentioned format.

    • Slots must align with available collection windows as per slot search API.

  3. Reason Requirements:

    • reasonText must be meaningful

request.description:
Description:

Retrieves diagnostic reports in either PDF or XML format. The API returns a pre-signed URL that provides access to the report file stored in secure storage.

Request Details

API-Specific Headers

Header Required Description
Authorization Yes Bearer token

Path Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
orderId String Yes Order identifier (starts with VLXXXXX)
leadId String Yes Lead reference number (starts with SPXXXXXX)

Query Parameters

Parameter Type Required Values Description
type String Yes PDF, XML Report format

Responses

Field:

Field Type Description
reportUrl String access URL

URL Types Explained

  1. S3 Pre-signed URL (PDF):

    • Direct access to PDF in secure storage

    • Typically includes security tokens in URL parameters

    • Expires after 10 minutes

    • Format: https://{bucket}.s3.{region}.amazonaws.com/{file-key}?...

    • Clients will receive a pre-signed S3 URL with webhook payload which will only be valid for 10 mins.
      Best practise: is to get the URL, download the report and store on their end itself.

  2. ASPX Endpoint (XML):

    • Direct access to XML (RAW) report

    • Requires additional processing by client. Sample of XML

    • Uses encrypted query parameters

    • Format: https://b2capi.thyrocare.com/API_BETA/ReportAccess.aspx?id={encrypted_ids}

request.description:
Webhook Integration Guide

Overview

Clients must provide a publicly accessible POST API endpoint where we will send order status updates for their orders. The updates will be triggered as specific order events occur on our system.

Webhook Request Details

Method & URL

  • Method: POST

  • URL: client_api_complete_url_with_domain_and_path (To be shared by the client)

Webhook Request Payload

Payload Format

Request Body can be in JSON or XML as per client preference.

Request Parameters

Field Data Type Description
orderId String Unique identifier of the order for which the update is being sent
timestamp String Event timestamp in format: "dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm" (24-hour format)
orderStatus String Latest order status for the provided orderId
b2cPatientId String Identifier for patient, usually a leadId starting with SP, if available
orderStatusDescription String Short description corresponding to the orderStatus
orderData Object Contains the full details of this order in the same format as returned by our Order Details API

Authentication (If Required)

  • If authentication is required, clients must:

    • Include an Authorization Header in the cURL request.

    • Use a static key-value pair for authentication (i.e., it must not change dynamically).

Order Status Mappings

Status Description
Yet to Assign ORDER_BOOKED
Assigned ORDER_ASSIGNED
Started PHLEBO_STARTED
Arrived PHLEBO_ARRIVED
Serviced SAMPLE_COLLECTED
Sample Imported SAMPLE_IMPORTED
Rescheduled ORDER_RESCHEDULED
Cancelled ORDER_CANCELLED
Done REPORT_FULL

IP Whitelisting Requirements

To ensure seamless connectivity between your network and our services, please whitelist the following static IP addresses in your network firewall if any:

  • Staging Environment: 43.204.243.10

  • Production Environment: 3.111.206.168

⚠️ Note: If your infrastructure is protected by a network firewall, you must whitelist the above IPs to allow secure communication between our environments and your systems. Failure to do so may result in connectivity issues or failed integration attempts.

These IPs are static and should be added to your firewall rules for uninterrupted integration.

Important Notes

  1. Only POST requests are supported.

  2. Endpoint must be publicly accessible.

  3. Timestamp format is strictly "dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm".

  4. API must handle requests asynchronously and respond within 1 second.

  5. Only order events occurring after webhook integration will trigger updates.

  6. For the "REPORT_FULL" event - clients will receive a pre-signed S3 URL with webhook payload which will only be valid for 10 mins.
    Best practise is to get the URL, download the report and store on their end itself.

  7. Clients must share the working cURL request before finalizing the integration.

item.name: FAQ - Best Practices
item.description:
1. What does the “Data Not Found” error in the Report API indicate?

This error typically occurs under the following circumstances:

  • The requested report data is older than three months and is no longer retained in the system.

  • The report has not yet been generated for the given order.

In such cases, clients are advised to check the report generation status using the Order Details API.

2. How can clients check the status of report generation?

To verify whether a report has been generated, clients should use the Order Details API.

Within the API response:

  • Check the field indicating report generation status in 'patient' field: isReportAvailable.

  • If the status is true, it is safe to proceed with invoking the Report API.

3. What is the difference between XML and PDF report formats?

PDF Report:

  • Intended for cases where a fully formatted and printable document is required.

  • Includes headers, footers, branding, and other visual elements.

  • The API response contains a reportUrl field, providing a direct link to download the PDF report.

XML Report:

  • Suitable for use cases where raw test data and values are required for custom parsing or report creation.

  • The response is delivered in a structured XML format. (A sample XML report is attached in Report API section)

4. What is the authorized and correct source for catalog or product information for any client?

Clients must always use the official Catalog API to retrieve the latest information.

This API serves as the single source of truth for all product data, including PSKUs, SSKUs, etc. and other catalog information.

5. When can an order be rescheduled or canceled after its creation?

Orders can be rescheduled or canceled only after a minimum of 3-4 minutes from the time of creation.
This intentional delay ensures that the post-order processing is complete and that the order data has been fully synchronized within internal systems.

6. Where can clients find the updated appointment date and time after rescheduling an order?

Clients can retrieve the latest appointment details using the Order Details API.

In the API response:

  • Navigate to the field: orderTracking → status: APPOINTMENT_DATE

    This field reflects the most recent appointment date and time after rescheduling.

item.name: API Integration Guide
item.description:
1. Introduction

This document outlines the step-by-step API integration flow for clients to implement the order booking system. The integration involves 12 APIs categorized under Authentication, Cart Management, Catalog Management, Serviceability, Order Management, and Report Management.

A flowchart is provided for better visualization of the integration process.

2. API Integration Flow

Step 1: Authentication

Before accessing any APIs, the client must authenticate using one of the following methods:

Option A: Login via Credentials

  • Hit Login API with valid credentials.

  • Receive an authentication token (JWT/Bearer Token).

Option B: Login via OTP

  1. Hit OTP Generation API (if not logged in).

  2. Verify OTP using OTP Verification API.

  3. Receive an authentication token.

Logout (Optional)

  • Use Logout API to invalidate the token.

Step 2: Fetch Product Catalog

  • Call Catalog API to retrieve available SKUs (products/services).

  • Display the list to the user for selection.

  • The "id" of each SKU is critical. Please pass this "id" in the Create Order API.

Step 3: Check Serviceability

  • Before booking, verify if the patient’s location is serviceable using Pincode Serviceability API.

  • If the pincode is serviceable, proceed to cart.

Step 4: Cart Management

  • Add patient details, product details, and verify pricing (payableAmount, incentives, etc.) using Cart API.

Step 5: Search Available Slots

  • Fetch available phlebo (technician) slots using Search Slots API.

  • Select a convenient appointment date & time.

Step 6: Create Order

  • Pass the sku details for each patient in the "items" array inside every "patient" object.

  • The "id" field inside the "items" array needs to be picked from Catalog API

  • Confirm booking by calling Create Order API.

  • Receive an order number (orderNo) in response.

Step 7: Order Management

Check Order Details

  • Retrieve order details using Order Details API (with orderNo).

List Orders

  • Fetch all booked orders within a date range using Order Listing API.

Cancel/Reschedule Order

  • Cancel an order using Order Cancellation API.

  • Reschedule an order using Order Reschedule API (before appointment).

Step 8: Report Management

  • Check isReportAvailable flag in Order Details API.

  • If true, fetch the report (PDF/XML) using Report API.

Real-Time Updates (Optional - Webhooks)

  • Integrate webhooks to receive real-time order status updates (avoid polling).

4. API Reference Summary

Category API Name Purpose
Authentication Login API Generate token via credentials
Logout API Invalidate token
OTP Generation API Generate OTP for login
OTP Verification API Verify OTP for login
Catalog Catalog API Fetch available SKUs
Serviceability Pincode Serviceability API Check if pincode is serviceable
Search Slots API Get available appointment slots
Cart Cart API Add/verify patient & product details
Order Management Create Order API Confirm booking & generate orderNo
Order Details API Fetch order details
Order Listing API List orders in a date range
Order Cancellation API Cancel an order
Order Reschedule API Reschedule an order
Reports Report API Download report (PDF/XML)

5. Best Practices

  1. Token Security: Always pass the token in the Authorization header.

  2. Error Handling: Check HTTP status codes (200, 400, 401, 500).

  3. Webhooks: Use for real-time updates instead of polling.

  4. Rate Limiting: Avoid hitting APIs repeatedly in short intervals.
